message:
chore(beads): file 5 no-mocks audit beads (cc_2 swarm-tick audit)

Applied the /testing-perfect-e2e-integration-tests-with-logging-and-no-mocks
skill to crates/frankenterm-core/tests/ + crates/frankenterm-gui/tests/.
Mock Risk Matrix (Production Impact × Divergence Risk):

  ft-dvgzi  snapshot_e2e.rs (MockWezterm in 2058-line "e2e")           25
  ft-voeqm  recorder_migration_labruntime.rs (MockMigrationStorage)    25
  ft-2funa  watchdog_labruntime.rs (FailingMockWezterm hides flakes)   16
  ft-i75v5  proptest_replay_artifact_registry.rs (MockFs trait)        12
  ft-geg03  webhook_labruntime.rs (MockTransport hides HTTP edges)     12

All 5 score ≥ 8 → MUST be mock-free per skill matrix. Each bead names
the real fixture available (WeztermClient subprocess, AppendLogRecorderStorage
with tempdir, std::fs+TempDir, httptest/wiremock) plus a suggested
incremental migration path that keeps existing mock tests green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -258,6 +258,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-g82hb","title":"[reality-gap] README config example advertises keys that parse but are silently ignored","description":"The README configuration example under `## Configuration` does not match the effective config surface of the shipped binary.

Evidence:
- A reconstructed snippet containing `workflows.enabled = true` fails `ft config validate` with `invalid type: boolean true, expected a sequence`.
- When rewritten as `enabled = []` so the file parses, `ft config export --path ... --json` drops or ignores several documented keys:
  - `workflows.concurrency` is ignored; effective config still uses `max_concurrent`.
  - `safety.approve_new_hosts` is ignored.
  - `safety.redact_secrets` is ignored.
  - `patterns.packs = ["core"]` parses, but the effective config surface uses builtin-qualified pack names.

This is dangerous because operators can paste the README block, get either a hard type error or a config that validates while silently not applying the documented knobs.

Expected fix:
- Update README to show only wired keys (`workflows.enabled` as a list, `workflows.max_concurrent`, real safety keys/nesting, builtin-qualified pattern packs).
- Optionally make the parser reject unknown keys instead of silently dropping them.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","created_by":"GreenWren","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","close_reason":"Fixed by narrowing the README config example to keys that the shipped parser actually wires: builtin-qualified pattern packs, list-shaped workflow enablement, `max_concurrent`, and real safety/redaction keys.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","reality-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-gbpoy","title":"[perf] codec serialize_with_mode Auto-path double-serializes large PDUs (serialize once for size check, then re-serialize through zstd encoder)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:11.974085Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:19:30.404615Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:19:30.404150Z","close_reason":"Shipped at HEAD 51101858. Replaced the inner re-serialize (varbincode::Serializer through a streaming zstd::Encoder) with zstd::stream::encode_all(&uncompressed[..], DEFAULT_COMPRESSION_LEVEL) — feeds the already-serialized buffer directly through zstd's bulk-stream API. Same wire output, half the serializer work above COMPRESS_THRESH. Also added Vec::with_capacity(64) for the uncompressed buffer (small alloc-grow win). Build: cargo check -p codec clean (24.47s). Tests: 10 codec serialize tests passed, 0 failed (serialize_auto_mode_large_data, serialize_always_mode, serialize_never_mode roundtrip all green). For PDUs above 32 bytes (COMPRESS_THRESH), expect ~50% reduction in serializer CPU on the encode path. No microsecond benchmark gathered in this slot — fix is structural and the test suite verifies wire compatibility.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.645671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.644374Z","close_reason":"Landed in 503c9506 (retry: exempt Error::Cancelled from circuit-breaker failure count) + 765743d5 (regression test) + 79029eeb (test fix: private fields -> snapshot()).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-geg03","title":"[no-mocks-test] crates/frankenterm-core/tests/webhook_labruntime.rs: MockTransport hides real HTTP timeout/retry/TLS bugs — use httptest or reqwest::Client to localhost","description":"## Mock at issue
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/webhook_labruntime.rs:
  33: struct MockRequest { ... }
  40: struct MockTransport { requests: Arc<Mutex<Vec<MockRequest>>> }
  65: impl WebhookTransport for MockTransport
  126, 154, 171, 188, 210, 235, 277, 319, ...: MockTransport::success() /
  MockTransport::failure(...) used throughout the test suite.

## Why the mock is risky
Webhooks are an outbound integration surface. The MockTransport returns
canned success/failure synchronously and ignores:
- Real HTTP request shape (headers, content-type, body framing) —
  if we generate a malformed body the mock accepts it
- Real timeout / connect-error / DNS-failure handling
- Retry/backoff semantics under transient network failure
- TLS / cert handling
- Slow consumer (server reads body slowly)
- Connection-pool exhaustion under burst load
- Partial-response failures (server crashes mid-body)
- Real Content-Length / Transfer-Encoding edge cases

The test claims "WebhookSender retries on 500" but cannot distinguish
"sender retries on the SemanticError that the mock returns" from
"sender retries on the real reqwest::Error that production sees."

Mock Risk score: 3 (integration outage if webhooks miss) × 4 (real
HTTP failure modes are an order of magnitude richer) = 12 → MUST be
mock-free.

## Real fixture available
- `httptest = "0.16"` (or `wiremock`) crate — local HTTP server in
  test process, asserts on actual HTTP traffic.
- Alternative: tokio TcpListener bound to 127.0.0.1:0, simple
  request/response loop in the test.
- The production webhook transport uses reqwest; reqwest::Client
  pointed at a localhost test server is a real HTTP roundtrip.

## Suggested approach
1. Add `httptest` or `wiremock` to [dev-dependencies] in
   crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml.
2. Migrate the success / 500 / 4xx / timeout / retry tests to drive
   real HTTP requests at a localhost test server.
3. Keep MockTransport ONLY for tests that probe the WebhookSender's
   internal queueing/dedup logic (those score < 8).
4. Add regression tests for the bugs the mock cannot catch:
   - prop: malformed JSON body → real server rejects with 400, not
     silent success
   - timeout: server holds the connection for 60s, sender must time
     out at the configured deadline
   - retry: server returns 500 on first request, 200 on second, sender
     succeeds within max_retries
5. JSON-line logging per request: ts, method, path, body bytes,
   status, ms.

## Acceptance
- ≥4 webhook tests drive real HTTP traffic at httptest/wiremock.
- Timeout regression test passes.
- Existing MockTransport tests still green where retained.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:12:32.753630Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:12:32.753630Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","no-mocks","test","webhook"]}
 {"id":"ft-gfawa","title":"frankenterm-core lib build blocked by web_framework asupersync type skew","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:10:44.813713Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:42:11.511779Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:42:11.511187Z","close_reason":"Fixed in efbf601c","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-gjjew","title":"retry loop retries operation-level cancellation instead of surfacing it","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:31:49.054890Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:38:18.464865Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:38:18.463876Z","close_reason":"Verified in 464ad9b4; further cargo proof blocked by ft-h6zod ingest.rs:2926 type inference regression","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-gk2ng","title":"runtime_async doctrine docs still treat runtime_compat as current API","description":"Reality-check finding after ft-y378j.4 removed the runtime_compat alias and scripts/check_runtime_compat_residuals.sh now scopes old-name references out of code. Several operator-facing doctrine/baseline docs still present runtime_compat as the active/current async API surface instead of historical context: docs/asupersync-migration-baseline.md:49-97 lists runtime_compat:: counts, highest-concentration file crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs, and canonical target mappings through runtime_compat; docs/adr/0012-asupersync-runtime-doctrine.md:36-51 says prefer runtime_compat/cx boundaries and maps tokio::time::* to runtime_compat::*; docs/asupersync-architecture-doctrine.md has the same active guidance. README.md:41 now says the deprecated alias has been removed and runtime_async is canonical, so these docs can steer agents back toward a removed API name even though CI only guards Rust source. Fix: refresh the doctrine/baseline docs to say runtime_async for current guidance, move runtime_compat counts into dated historical sections, and ensure validation docs distinguish historical migration evidence from current API names.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:50:02.856218Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T00:07:56.801645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T00:07:56.801399Z","close_reason":"Refreshed runtime doctrine docs to make runtime_async current and runtime_compat historical.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -289,6 +290,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-hsmjb","title":"Event::PaneDisappeared declared but never emitted from production runtime","description":"events.rs:181 declared Event::PaneDisappeared. event_stream filters, wire_protocol serializer, main.rs Web SSE route, and tests all matched on it — but no production code path in runtime.rs::handle_native_event ever called bus.publish(PaneDisappeared). Consumers remained dormant forever. Fix landed in 1f3ae436: publish on NativeEvent::PaneDestroyed guarded on event_bus.is_some(). Tests landed in 41cab3bb: ft_pp7jk_pane_destroyed_publishes_pane_disappeared_event + companion no-bus path. Symmetrical to Event::PaneDiscovered which was already published. Unlocks future long-lived subsystems (policy caches, connector bridges) that want per-pane state teardown.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:33.083454Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:24.250654Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:24.250393Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1f3ae436 (fix(runtime): publish Event::PaneDisappeared from handle_native_event) + 41cab3bb (tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-htf0i","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:57:00.591671Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.138080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.137486Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-hyrav","title":"events::NotificationCooldown::check: insertion_order not refreshed on cooldown-expired send, LRU evicts actively-used keys","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:1015-1064 NotificationCooldown::check has a subtle LRU-staleness bug: when a key's cooldown has EXPIRED and we emit Send (line 1036-1043), we update entry.last_notified to now and reset entry.suppressed_since_notify, but we do NOT move the key to the back of self.insertion_order. The key stays at its ORIGINAL insertion position. Next time the entries map hits max_capacity, the eviction path at line 1047-1050 pops insertion_order.pop_front() — which picks a key by ORIGINAL INSERTION ORDER, not by last-use. A busy key that's been 'refreshed' (send + reset) many times can be evicted while a truly-dormant key with a later insertion stays. Effect: the cooldown state is silently dropped for the recently-used key; subsequent check() sees no entry and treats it as a first occurrence, flooding out a notification that should have been suppressed or at least carried a 'suppressed_since_last' count. Same pattern already fixed in sibling EventDeduplicator at events.rs:867-870 (refresh insertion_order on window-expired reset). Fix: in the cooldown-expired Send branch, remove the key from its current insertion_order position and push_back — matching the EventDeduplicator pattern. Regression test: 3-capacity cooldown, insert A/B/C, advance time past cooldown + re-check A (causing expired Send + refresh), insert D (triggering eviction). Pre-fix A is evicted; post-fix B is evicted (truly oldest-by-use).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.607054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:50.222010Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:50.221646Z","close_reason":"Landed in 36c6f7d3 (fix(events): refresh NotificationCooldown insertion_order on cooldown-expired send). Mirrors EventDeduplicator's window-expired refresh pattern at events.rs:867-870. Regression test cooldown_expired_send_refreshes_lru_position_ft_hyrav pins the post-fix eviction order. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-i75v5","title":"[no-mocks-test] crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_replay_artifact_registry.rs: MockFs (test-only trait) hides fsync/metadata/race bugs — use tempdir + std::fs","description":"## Mock at issue
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_replay_artifact_registry.rs:
  43: struct MockFs { files: Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, Vec<u8>>> }
  59: impl FsBackend for MockFs
The MockFs is the only implementer of FsBackend in the workspace
(grep confirms no real FsBackend impl exists in src/), meaning the
trait abstraction was introduced solely for testing.

## Why the mock is risky
Replay artifacts (recordings, metadata, golden refs) are persistent
data. The MockFs uses a HashMap<PathBuf, Vec<u8>> and ignores:
- Filesystem metadata (mtime, size-on-disk vs len of bytes)
- Atomic-rename semantics (rename(temp, target))
- Fsync ordering — production code that writes-then-fsyncs is
  indistinguishable from the buggy write-without-fsync under MockFs
- Disk-full / EROFS / EACCES error paths
- Concurrent writer races (the Mutex serializes; real fs does not)
- Path-canonicalization edge cases (./, .., symlinks)

The artifact registry decides which replay artifacts are durable and
which to garbage-collect. A bug in size accounting under MockFs is
silent because the Mutex guarantees atomicity that real disk does not.

Mock Risk score: 3 (data: replay artifacts lost) × 4 (mock collapses
all I/O complexity) = 12 → MUST be mock-free.

## Real fixture available
std::fs + tempfile::TempDir. Trivial — already used in 72 other tests
under crates/frankenterm-core/tests/.

## Suggested approach
1. Inline-delete the FsBackend trait abstraction (test-only; no
   production impl exists). The artifact-registry production code
   already uses std::fs directly.
2. Update the proptests to drive the registry against a per-test
   tempfile::TempDir.
3. proptest's #![proptest_config(...)] already runs fast; tempdir
   create/teardown adds <1ms per case.
4. Add proptest properties the MockFs cannot exercise:
   - prop: writing artifact-A then crashing before sync still leaves
     a recoverable directory (we should detect partial writes via
     the `*.partial` rename pattern)
   - prop: concurrent writers on disjoint paths never corrupt either
5. Drop MockFs entirely once tests are migrated.

## Acceptance
- proptest_replay_artifact_registry.rs uses no MockFs.
- ≥10 properties run against tempdir.
- Suite wall time still < 5s.
- Crash-mid-write recovery property added.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:12:13.940045Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:12:13.940045Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifact-registry","no-mocks","replay","test"]}
 {"id":"ft-iaafy","title":"Robot state docs still show data.panes object envelope","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:09:27.406600Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:32:27.380996Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:32:27.379682Z","close_reason":"README.md + AGENTS.md now match actual Vec<PaneState> envelope; --include-text wrapper documented separately (537375d9)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3606,"issue_id":"ft-iaafy","author":"jemanuel","text":"Reality-check evidence (2026-04-23): docs still show ft robot state success as {"data":{"panes":[...]}} in README.md:74-84 and AGENTS.md:353-363. Actual handler returns RobotResponse::success(states, ...) for the non---include-text path at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:16885-16888, so success serializes data as a bare Vec<PaneState>, not an object with a panes field. Only the --include-text branch wraps panes in RobotStateWithTextData at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:16876-16884. Expected: docs/examples match the actual state success schema, or implementation changes back to data.panes.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:10:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-ibtjf","title":"[gh-52] build.rs must populate FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV for path-dep workspace layout","description":"## Problem (GitHub #52)

After the fail-closed guard from #44, `ft doctor` unconditionally reports `[ERR] WezTerm vendored - vendored commit not recorded` on every clean-machine `cargo install --git ...` build. Root cause:

- `crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs` only extracts vendored commit from `Cargo.lock` entries whose `source = "git+...#<hash>"` fragment carries a commit.
- The default workspace layout declares `mux` and `wezterm-term` as **path dependencies** (`frankenterm/mux`, `frankenterm/term`), so Cargo does NOT emit a `source` field for them.
- `select_vendored_metadata` returns `(None, None, None)`, no `cargo:rustc-env=FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV=...` is emitted, `option_env!("FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV")` resolves to `None`, and `vendored.rs` reports `Incompatible` at runtime.

Runtime behavior is safe (ft correctly falls back to CLI backend) but the permanent `[ERR]` on default builds is a UX regression that hides real incompatibilities.

## Fix direction

In `crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs`, add a path-dep fallback branch to `select_vendored_metadata`:

1. If `mux.source.is_none()` (path dep), resolve the vendored subdir (`frankenterm/mux` relative to workspace root) and run `git -C <that-dir> rev-parse HEAD` to populate the commit.
2. Handle the case where the workspace is not a git checkout (e.g. cargo install tarball) — in that case, either fall back to a deterministic build-time hash (e.g. `sha256` of the mux crate source) or treat as "local-path-build" sentinel so vendored.rs can accept it without [ERR].
3. Add `cargo:rerun-if-changed=` entries for `frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml` and `frankenterm/term/Cargo.toml` so the metadata refreshes on vendored bumps.
4. Emit `FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_VERSION` + `FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_SOURCE` in the path-dep branch too.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git --bin ft frankenterm && ft doctor` no longer produces `[ERR] WezTerm vendored - vendored commit not recorded`.
2. `ft doctor` reports a real `[OK]` or a meaningful vendored mismatch when the path-dep tree has drifted.
3. Runtime vendored compatibility guard (`vendored.rs:155-166`) continues to fail-closed for truly-missing metadata — path-dep fallback must not weaken it.
4. Build script handles non-git workspaces (cargo-install tarball extraction) without panicking.
5. Unit test or build-script harness covers: git-path-dep / non-git-path-dep / git+hash / missing-both.

## Reporter

GitHub user JordanChoo — https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm/issues/52","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:27:51.238506Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:50:52.805074Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T02:50:52.804717Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 3174399d (build.rs path-dep fallback + non-git sentinel + 8-test harness)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-icreu","title":"[MEDIUM] ActivityProfile::new allows NaN alpha/default_activity past clamp; poisons all hourly_ewma slots","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:437-438` — `ActivityProfile::new`.

ft-26k3h just hardened `update_hour` against NaN input. The construction-time path is the **complement**: `alpha.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` and `default_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` both still let NaN through because `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN.

Two independent failure modes:

1. **NaN `default_activity`**: all 24 `hourly_ewma` slots are initialised to NaN. `predict_hour` returns NaN for every slot until the first clean `update_hour` overwrites the bucket via the `sample_count == 0` branch. Until then, every hazard/survival score computed from `predict` silently produces NaN (matches the ft-26k3h failure shape but from a different path).

2. **NaN `alpha`**: `self.alpha = NaN`. `update_hour` at line 460 computes `alpha.mul_add(sample, (1.0 - alpha) * prev) = NaN` on every non-first sample. `hourly_ewma[idx]` stays NaN from the second sample onward. ft-26k3h's entry guard doesn't catch this because the caller passes a clean `normalized_activity` — the NaN is stored in `self.alpha`, not in the input.

Both are the same `f64::clamp(NaN)` gotcha shipped across ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce / ft-26k3h. This closes the pair's construction-time counterpart.

## Fix

Snap NaN to sensible defaults at the boundary:
- `alpha` → 0.1 (a conservative EWMA coefficient; config-adjacent code uses 0.3 as the common default but 0.1 is safe for an unspecified sentinel).
- `default_activity` → 0.5 (neutral midpoint — if operator didn't supply a meaningful default, 0.5 is the least-surprising fallback).

Both `.is_nan()` checks happen **before** the clamp so the clamp still trims out-of-range finite inputs normally.

## Tests

* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_alpha` — alpha=NaN → stored α=0.1, EWMA updates stay finite.
* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_default_activity` — default=NaN → all hourly_ewma slots = 0.5, predict_hour returns 0.5.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:55:55.543754Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:58:20.995785Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:58:20.995504Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
